definition: Self, especially as the imagined owner, controller, or identity claimed in relation to the heaps.
notes: The project keeps self as the default because it is direct and doctrinally serviceable. Real self is a controlled explanatory alternate when the passage is contrasting a presumed essence with not-self analysis, and owner is acceptable when the local wording emphasizes possession or control. The term should not be softened into vague identity-language where the text is directly making or rejecting a self-claim, but it also should not be inflated into soul unless the passage is explicitly about that stronger doctrinal idea. In the five-heaps family, attā matters mainly through the formulas that take form, felt experience, recognition, putting-together activities, or knowing as self. The OSF glossary and SN 22.59 support keeping self-language explicit here so that anattā remains a practical rejection of appropriation rather than an abstract slogan.

context_rules.context: In direct self-claim or self-view language in heap analysis
When the passage contrasts an imagined essence with not-self analysis
context_rules.notes: Use sparingly when English needs the reifying force made explicit.

context_rules.context: In formulas emphasizing possession or control over the heaps
context_rules.notes: Allowed explanatory alternate when the local syntax foregrounds owning or mastering.

example_phrases.notes: Part of the practical not-self refrain applied to the heaps.

version_notes: Promoted from a thin minor entry to a rule-bearing major entry aligned with anattā, sakkāya, and the self-view formulas applied to the heaps.

authority_basis.scope: Supports retaining plain self-language and discouraging both soul-language and modern ego-language as defaults.
Supports the practical self/not-self formulas applied across all five heaps.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'self' as the default rendering in direct doctrinal contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not inflate the term into 'soul' without textual warrant, and do not blur direct self-claims into softer identity language when the passage is making or rejecting a self-view.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift between self-language, identity-language, and not-self analysis in the heaps cluster.
